title: Fantasy TV
subtitle: Epic fantasy, mythological adaptations, prestige genre TV.

items.title: Game of Thrones
items.seasons: S1-S6 are peak; S7 weakened; S8 widely considered a failure
items.pitch: HBO. The fantasy show that defined a decade of TV. Westeros, the Iron Throne, the dragons.

critics.quote: Won 59 Emmys. The S8 finale arc remains one of the most-discussed quality collapses in TV history.

items.title: American Gods
items.seasons: S1 visually astonishing; quality collapsed after Fuller departure
items.pitch: Gaiman novel adaptation. Cross-listed Drama TV.

sections.name: II. High Priority
sections.desc: Strongest fits given GoT love + your fantasy film loves.

items.title: House of the Dragon
items.whyPriority: GoT prequel. Targaryen civil war. Tighter focus than GoT, single-house drama, dragon-heavy. Critically considered the best HBO fantasy since GoT peak.
items.pitch: 200 years before GoT. The Targaryens turn on each other. The Dance of Dragons.

critics.quote: Critical and commercial hit. Considered the Targaryen show GoT fans wanted.

items.title: Shōgun
items.whyPriority: Strongest single non-GoT fantasy/historical recommendation. FX/Hulu. James Clavell adaptation. 1600s feudal Japan. Won 18 Emmys including Outstanding Drama. Sandwich of historical drama and political maneuver — direct fit for your structural-rigor pillar.
items.pitch: An English navigator shipwrecks in feudal Japan and becomes pawn in a Lord Toranaga's bid for shogunate.

critics.quote: Won 18 Emmys including Outstanding Drama Series — the most ever for a single season.

items.title: The Witcher
items.seasons: Cavill departed after S3; S4-5 with Liam Hemsworth
items.whyPriority: Netflix. Sapkowski novels (also a video game franchise). Cavill's first three seasons land. The post-Cavill era is uncertain.
items.pitch: A monster hunter, a princess, and a sorceress on a continent of warring kingdoms.

critics.quote: S1-S3 considered solid epic fantasy; the post-Cavill direction is divisive.

items.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Rings of Power
items.whyPriority: Amazon. Most expensive TV show ever made. Second Age of Middle-earth. Critically polarizing on S1; S2 received better reviews.
items.pitch: Thousands of years before Bilbo and Frodo. The forging of the Rings. Sauron's rise.

critics.quote: S1 critically mixed. S2 widely considered an improvement.

items.title: His Dark Materials
items.whyPriority: BBC/HBO. Pullman trilogy adaptation. Daemon companions, parallel worlds, anti-religious-authoritarian fantasy. Faithful and serious.
items.pitch: An orphaned girl in a parallel-world Oxford discovers a conspiracy involving stolen children and a substance called Dust.

critics.quote: Critically warm; considered the strong adaptation that the 2007 film failed to be.

items.title: The Wheel of Time
items.pitch: Amazon. Robert Jordan novels. Critically polarizing — book purists divided. S2-S3 were stronger than S1.

critics.quote: Cancelled after S3. Reception throughout was mixed-to-positive among critics, polarizing among book fans.

items.title: Hannibal
items.whyPriority: Per your request. Cross-listed primary in Horror TV. Listed here because Fuller's visual approach is more fantasy-painting than horror-procedural. NBC astoundingly let him make this on network.
items.pitch: FBI profiler Will Graham works with psychiatrist Hannibal Lecter to catch killers. Graham doesn't yet know what Lecter is.

critics.quote: Routinely cited as one of the most visually beautiful TV shows ever made on network.

items.title: Carnival Row
items.pitch: Amazon. Victorian noir-fantasy. Mythological refugees in a steampunk city. Orlando Bloom, Cara Delevingne. Underseen.

critics.quote: Mixed critical reception; cult fan base.

items.title: Outlander
items.whyPriority: Ronald D. Moore (BSG showrunner). Diana Gabaldon novels. Time-travel-romance-fantasy hybrid. Period 18th-century Scotland adventure.
items.pitch: 1945 British nurse is transported to 1743 Scotland and entangled in the Jacobite rebellion.

critics.quote: Long-running critical favorite. Caitríona Balfe Globe nomination several times.

items.title: Penny Dreadful
items.pitch: Cross-listed Horror TV. Victorian London, gothic literature monsters mash-up. Eva Green carries it.

critics.quote: Critical favorite; ratings struggled. Green Globe nomination.

items.title: The Sandman
items.pitch: Netflix. Gaiman comic adaptation. Tom Sturridge as Dream of the Endless. Anthology-flavored within a long arc.

critics.quote: S1 critically warm. S2 ended the show.

items.title: Black Sails
items.pitch: Starz. Pirate-era Caribbean. A Treasure Island prequel. Surprisingly literary, character-driven. Underseen.

critics.quote: Critical reputation grew steadily; cult devotion strong.

items.title: The Last Kingdom
items.runtime: 5 seasons · 46 episodes + Seven Kings Must Die film
items.pitch: BBC/Netflix. Cornwell's Saxon Stories adaptation. 9th-century England, Vikings vs. Saxons. Procedurally tighter than Vikings.

critics.quote: Critical favorite throughout run. Considered superior to Vikings by many medieval-historical-drama devotees.

items.title: Vikings
items.pitch: History Channel. Ragnar Lothbrok and Norse expansion. S1-S4 considered peak.

critics.quote: Hugely successful internationally; critical reputation modest.

items.title: Good Omens
items.pitch: Amazon/BBC. Pratchett-Gaiman novel adaptation. David Tennant + Michael Sheen as a demon and angel preventing Armageddon.

critics.quote: Critical favorite. Tennant/Sheen chemistry the show.
